verses.commentary: Paul's methodology matched his message. Refusing eloquence or human wisdom, he let the testimony of God stand on its own power.
Paul's deliberate decision to know nothing except Christ crucified was strategic, not intellectual limitation. The cross alone suffices.
Weakness, fear, and trembling marked Paul's arrival. Divine power manifests best through human inadequacy, not confident ability.
Paul's preaching avoided persuasive wisdom, relying instead on Spirit demonstration and power. Method must match message.
Faith built on human wisdom will fail when that wisdom is challenged. Faith resting on God's power endures.
Among the mature, Paul does speak wisdom, but not the wisdom of this age or its rulers who are fading. True wisdom is reserved for those who have grown.
God's wisdom was hidden as mystery, predestined before creation for our glory. What seems new was planned eternally.
The rulers' ignorance of God's wisdom led them to crucify the Lord of glory. Had they known, they would not have accomplished God's saving purpose.
Quoting Isaiah 64:4, Paul emphasizes that God's prepared blessings exceed all human imagination and conception. What awaits believers surpasses comprehension.
The Spirit reveals these incomprehensible things. The Spirit searches even God's deep things, accessing and communicating divine realities.
Only a person's own spirit knows their thoughts. Analogously, only God's Spirit knows God's thoughts. Divine knowledge requires divine revelation.
Believers receive not the world's spirit but God's Spirit, enabling understanding of what God has freely given. Reception of the Spirit changes epistemology.
Spirit taught words explain spiritual realities. Both content and expression come from the Spirit, not human origin.
The natural person lacks capacity to receive spiritual things, considering them foolishness. Spiritual discernment requires spiritual life.
The spiritual person judges all things but is judged by no one. Spiritual perception gives insight that natural wisdom cannot evaluate or understand.
Who has known the Lord's mind to instruct him? Yet believers have Christ's mind. The indwelling Spirit grants participation in Christ's perspective.
